name: Stuffed Peppers

ingredients.item: can San Marzano tomatoes plus 1 can water

method: would a pumpkin.) Cut off the stems and chop up the pepper tops. Parboil the peppers. Parboil is boiling the pepper in water to cook it halfway and set aside. In a large frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T olive oil, add onion and saute until transparent. Add ground beef, break it up with a wooden spoon until cooked. Add tomatoes, water, and uncooked arborio rice and stir. Add oregano, salt, pepper, parsley, and crushed garlic that you will remove it later, and stir again. Simmer covered for 20 minutes, check on it often. If the rice is absorbing too fast add a bit more water and stir. In the meantime, arrange your peppers on a parchment-lined baking sheet. When the rice is cooked al dente and the water is absorbed, add pecorino cheese and mozzarella to the meat/rice/sauce mixture, mix well. Preheat oven to 350° F. Fill each pepper to the top. Add grated pecorino (abundantly). Bake covered with aluminum foil for 20 minutes. Remove foil and cook an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the tops are brown and cheese is melted. Serving Suggestion: fresh bread and salad. Serves 2-3. Olé
